fetch_unit.designer_hint: Fetch-stage event; inspect PC update, branch redirect, and stall hold behavior.
fetch_unit.likely_fix_pattern: Gate fetch progress when no instruction can retire, preserving branch redirect priority.

instr_rom.designer_hint: Instruction ROM event; inspect read-enable and address hold behavior.
instr_rom.likely_fix_pattern: Separate ROM read enable from PC/address hold during stalls.

decode_unit.designer_hint: Decode-stage event; inspect control decode holds during stalls.
decode_unit.likely_fix_pattern: Hold decode controls during non-progress stalls.

execute_unit.designer_hint: Execute-stage event; inspect ALU, branch, WFI, and LSU request generation.
execute_unit.likely_fix_pattern: Gate execute control and operands when held by LSU backpressure.

regfile.designer_hint: Register-file event; inspect unnecessary reads and load writeback timing.
regfile.likely_fix_pattern: Avoid register-file access when an instruction is held or squashed.

load_store_unit.designer_hint: LSU event; inspect request, response, and stall propagation.
load_store_unit.likely_fix_pattern: Keep one request per memory instruction and freeze front-end state while waiting.

data_bus_interconnect.designer_hint: Data bus event; inspect address decode and selected slave behavior.
data_bus_interconnect.likely_fix_pattern: Only decode and route when a request handshake is active.

data_sram.designer_hint: Data SRAM event; inspect load/store mix and memory-side clock enable.
data_sram.likely_fix_pattern: Keep SRAM access enabled only for accepted memory transactions.

dataflow_unit.designer_hint: Dataflow event; inspect MMIO control overhead versus useful MAC activity.
dataflow_unit.likely_fix_pattern: Amortize MMIO control and split control/datapath clock enables.

power_controller.designer_hint: Power-controller event; inspect state residency and recovery overhead.
power_controller.likely_fix_pattern: Choose deeper idle states only when residency amortizes recovery energy.

load_store_unit.stall_cycle.designer_hint: Check the LSU outstanding transaction state machine and pipeline stall propagation.
load_store_unit.stall_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Freeze front-end stages during LSU wait and prevent duplicate request issue.

load_store_unit.request_issue.designer_hint: Check that one architectural load/store issues one data-side request.
load_store_unit.request_issue.likely_fix_pattern: Use a single-outstanding FSM with an explicit accepted-request state.

load_store_unit.response_complete.designer_hint: Check load writeback and store retirement only occur after response completion.
load_store_unit.response_complete.likely_fix_pattern: Retire the held memory instruction only when response_valid is asserted.

fetch_unit.pc_update.designer_hint: Check PC enable during stalls and branch redirect priority.
fetch_unit.pc_update.likely_fix_pattern: Gate PC update during non-progress stalls while preserving branch redirect priority.

fetch_unit.fetch_ce_cycle.designer_hint: Check whether fetch clock enable drops during LSU wait states.
fetch_unit.fetch_ce_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Drive fetch_ce from valid progress, not only from the parent core clock.

fetch_unit.stall_valid_cycle.designer_hint: Valid fetch work is present while progress is blocked by a stall.
fetch_unit.stall_valid_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Hold PC and fetch-stage state during LSU wait states.

decode_unit.decode_instruction.designer_hint: Check decode register enable and decode control hold during stalls.
decode_unit.decode_instruction.likely_fix_pattern: Hold decode controls during stall and preserve flush priority.

decode_unit.stall_valid_cycle.designer_hint: Decode contains valid work while no instruction can retire.
decode_unit.stall_valid_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Disable decode register updates during LSU wait states.

execute_unit.stall_valid_cycle.designer_hint: Execute contains valid work while the LSU is draining a request.
execute_unit.stall_valid_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Disable execute operand/control updates during LSU wait states.

instr_rom.instruction_fetch.designer_hint: Check whether the instruction ROM is read during stalled cycles.
instr_rom.instruction_fetch.likely_fix_pattern: Disable instruction ROM read during non-progress stall cycles.

instr_rom.stall_hold_cycle.designer_hint: The ROM address is held while the front end waits for the LSU.
instr_rom.stall_hold_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Add an explicit instruction ROM read enable separate from address hold.

data_bus_interconnect.address_decode.designer_hint: Check MMIO/SRAM transaction counts and address decode activity.
data_bus_interconnect.address_decode.likely_fix_pattern: Decode address only when a request handshake is active or transaction is accepted.

data_bus_interconnect.mmio_route.designer_hint: MMIO traffic is being routed to the dataflow control aperture.
data_bus_interconnect.mmio_route.likely_fix_pattern: Amortize control traffic with descriptor, repeat-count, FIFO, or doorbell semantics.

data_bus_interconnect.sram_route.designer_hint: SRAM requests are routed through the tiny data-side interconnect.
data_bus_interconnect.sram_route.likely_fix_pattern: Keep SRAM request path single-issued unless a future cache/miss path is added.

dataflow_unit.mmio_access.designer_hint: Check CPU control traffic into the dataflow block.
dataflow_unit.mmio_access.likely_fix_pattern: Add descriptor/repeat-count mode to amortize MMIO control.

dataflow_unit.operand_write.designer_hint: Operand writes show CPU setup traffic before useful dataflow work.
dataflow_unit.operand_write.likely_fix_pattern: Use a repeat-count, FIFO, or scratchpad path so operands are not rewritten for each MAC.

dataflow_unit.command_write.designer_hint: Command writes are control doorbells from the CPU.
dataflow_unit.command_write.likely_fix_pattern: Make command writes pulse-based and amortize them over multiple MAC operations.

dataflow_unit.status_read.designer_hint: Status polling can consume CPU, LSU, and bus energy.
dataflow_unit.status_read.likely_fix_pattern: Prefer done interrupts, batched polling, or descriptor completion flags for larger bursts.

dataflow_unit.result_read.designer_hint: Result reads are required, but should be amortized over a useful burst.
dataflow_unit.result_read.likely_fix_pattern: Read results once per burst or descriptor, not once per scalar operation.

dataflow_unit.mac_accumulate.designer_hint: Useful accelerator work; compare this against control traffic and idle cycles.
dataflow_unit.mac_accumulate.likely_fix_pattern: Keep MAC datapath enabled only when op_valid or local repeat work is active.

dataflow_unit.mac_active_cycle.designer_hint: MAC datapath is doing useful work.
dataflow_unit.mac_active_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Use this as the useful-active numerator for datapath clock-enable efficiency.

dataflow_unit.idle_cycle.designer_hint: Dataflow is clocked while not producing MAC work.
dataflow_unit.idle_cycle.likely_fix_pattern: Split MMIO control enable from MAC datapath clock enable.

dataflow_unit.done_assert.likely_fix_pattern: Keep done status observable even when the MAC datapath clock enable is off.

power_controller.mode_transition.designer_hint: Check mode transition count and recovery-energy share.
power_controller.mode_transition.likely_fix_pattern: Only use deeper power states when residency amortizes recovery cost.
